description: Nodes, edges, and claims for knowledge graph model testing

nodes.title: Polymorphic Rendering
nodes.definition: UI pattern where each content type receives its own visual treatment based on its type identity.

nodes.title: Content Type System
nodes.definition: Backend architecture where different content types share a queryable base but carry type-specific fields.

nodes.title: Type preservation enables rendering
nodes.statement: If the backend flattens everything into a base class, the frontend loses the type signal needed for polymorphic rendering.

nodes.title: Performance vs Type Fidelity
nodes.description: django-polymorphic provides type fidelity but at the cost of ContentType JOINs on every query. non_polymorphic() sacrifices fidelity for speed.

edges.reason: Polymorphic rendering requires the backend to preserve type identity through the query layer.
The claim directly argues that type preservation is necessary for the rendering pattern.
The tension exists within the implementation of the content type system.
